behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (dispatcher -> reviewer clients), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network behavior is required to catch wrong payload wiring. LLM-DEP: correctness here is about request construction, not model judgment. MULTI-STEP: not needed. Adversarial critique: if dispatch mapping is implemented in one pure function, Unit might be enough; I keep Integration because the real risk is miswiring across role routing + payload assembly modules. Rejection gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text only requires 'sends ... correct ... instruction pack' (invocation content), not live model output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with three in-process reviewer adapters (test doubles) and assert each adapter receives the identical document bytes plus the role-specific instruction pack; fail if any role gets wrong doc/pack pairing.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is model output quality (extraction accuracy), which a stub would always hide. REAL-INFRA may exist, but the minimum failure boundary is the real model decision itself. MULTI-STEP: single reviewer decision, so not Workflow. Adversarial critique: extraction errors could come from prompt templating bugs (Integration), but behavior explicitly says 'live clause extractor identifies key terms,' which requires real model behavior. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior is one agentic step, not a multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live clause extractor on a curated real contract set with known key terms and assert required entities are present (allowing deterministic normalization); treat misses on parties/dates/obligations/penalties as failures.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at boundary between config loader/orchestrator and risk-assessor client. LLM-DEP: not about whether model judges risk correctly, only whether policy payload is forwarded. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with in-process adapter capture. Adversarial critique: if policy mapping is a pure serializer, Unit could catch it; Integration remains minimum because the defect often occurs when config binding + invoker wiring interact. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is 'configuration is passed correctly' (transport), not output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with a non-default policy fixture and a capturing risk-assessor adapter; assert invocation contains exact policy id/rules/version expected by runtime config.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on semantic interpretation by the real model (especially indirect/euphemistic language). Stubs hide this failure class. MULTI-STEP: single assessor output, so not Workflow. Adversarial critique: could fail from wrong policy routing (Integration), but behavior explicitly tests whether live assessor flags nuanced language, which is model-judgment dependent. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because no sequential multi-agent dependence is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use live risk assessor on contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses under a fixed policy; assert risky clauses are flagged with expected severity bands.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: defect is in data handoff across modules (context builder -> compliance client). LLM-DEP: not checking compliance judgment quality, only invocation parameters. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed if adapter captures payload. Adversarial critique: Unit might suffice if this is a pure argument builder; Integration is safer because misbinding often occurs at wiring boundaries. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is strictly about passing jurisdiction/profile, not model decision accuracy.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with explicit jurisdiction/profile fixtures and a capturing compliance-checker adapter; assert outgoing invocation exactly matches expected jurisdiction code and regulatory profile fields.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is substantive compliance determination by the live model, not just transport/retry logic. A mock would hide reasoning failures. MULTI-STEP: this behavior targets one reviewer decision, so Agentic not Workflow. Adversarial critique: some failures could be deterministic rules-engine bugs (Integration), but behavior explicitly requires 'live compliance checker correctly determines,' so real model output must be evaluated. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text does not require chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate live compliance checker on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes for the configured regulations; assert decisions match expected outcomes within predefined tolerance.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is merge logic on provided inputs; can be reproduced in a single merge function/object without module I/O. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none (inputs are fixed reviewer outputs). MULTI-STEP: not required to validate merge rule itself. Adversarial critique: if attribution metadata is injected by upstream modules, Integration might be needed; behavior text scopes to 'merge step preserves both assessments with attribution,' which is local merge behavior. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because no concrete behavior text requires cross-module runtime interactions beyond supplying deterministic inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with two synthetic reviewer findings on the same clause that disagree, then assert output contains both assessments and explicit source attribution for each.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: requires multiple agentic steps in sequence/parallel (three live reviewers) plus merge orchestration. LLM-DEP: each reviewer output is model-dependent. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing reviewers can miss loss/drop issues triggered by real asynchronous agent outputs and variability. Adversarial critique: an Integration test with deterministic stubs could catch some loss bugs; however behavior explicitly demands success 'on a real contract' across full reviewer pipeline, which keeps Workflow minimum. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because this is not a single model decision; it is coordinated multi-agent pipeline behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on a real contract using live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers, then verify unified report contains all findings from each reviewer (no dropped clauses/flags) and preserves merge artifacts.
